source_fidelity.origin: Codex session JSONL generated by the real runtime
source_fidelity.why_faithful: These numbers come from Codex structured runtime logs for the real session, not from local estimation or approximate tokenization done by this script.
source_fidelity.limitation: The credit and USD calculations depend on the official rates mapped in this script and assume standard pricing; if Speed mode or another special pricing mode was used and is not visible in the JSONL, that will not be reflected here.

session.started_at_description: Session start timestamp recorded in session_meta.timestamp within the Codex session JSONL.
session.model_description: Active model taken from turn_context/session JSONL when available; not estimated.
session.plan_type_description: Subscription plan recorded by Codex for the session (for example: plus, pro, business).
session.model_context_window_description: Maximum model context window reported by Codex for this session.

latest_execution.recorded_at_description: Timestamp of the latest token_count event used for this latest execution block.
latest_execution.description: Latest usage block reported by Codex inside this session. It is the best faithful approximation of the latest billed/countable execution chunk recorded in the JSONL.

input_tokens.description: Input tokens reported by Codex for the usage block. They represent the prompt/context sent to the model for that block.

cached_input_tokens.description: Input tokens reused from cache. They are reported separately to show how much of the input was cache-backed. In these Codex reports they usually behave as an informational subset of input, not an extra amount to add again to total_tokens.

uncached_input_tokens.description: Derived field: input_tokens - cached_input_tokens. This approximates the portion of input that was not served from cache.

output_tokens.description: Output tokens generated by the model for the reported usage block.

reasoning_output_tokens.description: Informational output subfield reported by Codex for reasoning tokens. It is usually part of output_tokens, not an additional amount.

visible_output_tokens.description: Derived field: output_tokens - reasoning_output_tokens. This approximates the non-reasoning / externally visible output portion.

total_tokens.description: Total tokens reported by Codex for that block. In the observed examples it matches input_tokens + output_tokens; it does not add cached_input_tokens or reasoning_output_tokens again.

pricing_notes: Both credits_standard and price_usd_api_standard include cached_input_tokens as a separately billed component using the lower cached-input rate, not the standard input rate.
Credits are computed with the official token-based Codex rate card. If the task used Speed mode, this value may be understated because Speed has separate rates.
USD is shown as a standard API pricing reference, not as ChatGPT/Codex billing.

session_total.description: Total accumulated usage reported by Codex for the session up to the last token_count event found.
